channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform
item.description: Team8, Index Ventures, Picture Capital, Elad Gil, Cerca Partners, and Tesonet invested in Frame Security. The post Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform appeared first on Se...

item.title: GitGuardian Now Flags Admin and Overprivileged Identities Across AWS, Entra, and Okta
item.description: GitGuardian's NHI Governance now adds privilege context to leaked secrets, auto-escalating admin-level risks for smarter prioritization across AWS, Entra, and Okta. Discover how admin badges and overprivilege detectio...

item.title: Zimperium Mobile App Response Agent helps security teams counter mobile attacks
item.description: Zimperium launched Mobile App Response Agent, enabling security teams to respond faster than ever before to fraud and security threats. Leveraging Zimperium’s expertise in mobile security, Mobile App Response Agent is...

item.title: Anthropic’s bug-hunting Mythos was greatest marketing stunt ever, says cURL creator
item.description: After all that hype, AI scanner found one low-severity cURL flaw

item.title: Red Hat extends open source technology into space
item.description: Red Hat and Voyager Technologies announced the successful deployment of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.1 and Red Hat Universal Base Image (UBI) to Voyager’s LEOcloud Space Edge IaaS Micro Datacenter aboard the Internatio...

item.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that it identified an unknown threat actor using a zero-day exploit that it said was likely developed with an artificial intelligence (AI) system, marking the first time the technology has b...

item.title: Instructure confirms hackers used Canvas flaw to deface portals
item.description: Education technology giant Instructure has confirmed that a security vulnerability allowed hackers to modify Canvas login portals and leave an extortion message. [...]

item.title: Poor security left hackers inside water company network for nearly two years
item.description: The UK’s data protection regulator, the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO), fined South Staffordshire Water’s parent company £963,900 over security failures linked to a cyberattack that exposed the personal data...

item.title: TrickMo Variant Routes Android Trojan Traffic Through TON
item.description: ThreatFabric finds new TrickMo Android banking trojan variant routing C2 through The Open Network

item.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
item.description: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.

item.title: Rushed Patches Follow Broken Embargo on New Linux Kernel Vulnerabilities
item.description: Two new high-severity vulnerabilities, dubbed ’Dirty Frag’ when chained, have been found in the Linux kernel, affecting most Linux distributions

item.title: Why we use CAPTCHAs, (Mon, May 11th)
item.description: A few months ago, I implemented Cloudflare&#;x26;#;39;s Turnstile CAPTCHA on some pages. The reason for implementing these CAPTCHAs is obvious: Bots make up a large percentage of traffic and affect site performance.

item.title: Google warns artificial intelligence is accelerating cyberattacks and zero-day exploits
item.description: Google says hackers now use AI to create exploits, automate attacks, evade defenses, and target AI supply chains at scale. Artificial intelligence is rapidly changing the cyber threat landscape, and a new report from...

item.title: Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Rather than scanning code alone, Build Application Firewalls inspect runtime behavior inside the software build pipeline. The post Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack appeared first on...

item.title: GTIG AI Threat Tracker: Adversaries Leverage AI for Vulnerability Exploitation, Augmented Operations, and Initial Access
item.description: Executive Summary Since our February 2026 report on AI-related threat activity, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has continued to track a maturing transition from nascent AI-enabled operations to the industrial...

item.title: Why Changing Passwords Doesn’t End an Active Directory Breach
item.description: Resetting a password doesn't always remove attackers from Active Directory. Specops Software explains how cached credentials and Kerberos tickets can keep attackers authenticated after a reset. [...]

item.title: Google Detects First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit
item.description: The zero-day was designed to bypass 2FA and it was developed by a prominent cybercrime group. The post Google Detects First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
item.description: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.

item.title: Wiz at Wiz: Reducing Risk through Service Ownership
item.description: How Wiz security uses Service Catalog to turn cloud risk into service ownership

item.title: Alation AI Governance creates a system of record for AI oversight
item.description: Alation has introduced Alation AI Governance, a new offering that gives enterprises the system of record they are missing for AI compliance. Enterprises are deploying AI models, agents, and tools faster than they can...

item.title: Linux developers weigh emergency “killswitch” for vulnerable kernel functions
item.description: Linux kernel developers are reviewing a proposal for an emergency risk mitigation mechanism (“Killswitch”) that would allow administrators to disable vulnerable kernel functions at runtime. The proposal, submitted by...

item.title: Final Countdown: Last Chance to Join the Rapid7 Global Cybersecurity Summit
item.description: The Rapid7 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit is just around the corner, and with it, a final opportunity to join the conversations shaping how security teams are adapting to a rapidly changing landscape. Over the past...

item.title: SailPoint Agentic Fabric expands identity governance to autonomous AI agents
item.description: SailPoint has introduced SailPoint Agentic Fabric, a new platform designed to help enterprises secure AI agents and other non-human identities at scale. As organizations deploy autonomous AI agents across cloud enviro...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Rootkit, macOS Crypto Stealer, WebSocket Skimmers and More
item.description: Rough Monday. Somebody poisoned a trusted download again, somebody else turned cloud servers into public housing, and a few crews are still getting into boxes with bugs that should’ve died years ago — the same old hol...

item.title: Webinar this week: Prevention alone is not enough against modern attacks
item.description: This upcoming webinar explores how organizations need to combine security, backups, and recovery planning to reduce the impact of modern cyberattacks. [...]

item.title: Police take down relaunched criminal marketplace with 22,000 users, €3.6 million in revenue
item.description: German authorities shut down a relaunched version of the criminal marketplace Crimenetwork and arrested its suspected operator. The domain seizure notice (Source: BKA) A special unit of the Spanish National Police arr...

item.title: Welcome to the Google Summer of Code 2026!
item.description: On behalf of the entire OWASP Foundation, it is our absolute pleasure to welcome 26 new contributors to Google Summer of Code 2026. This summer, you’re not just writing code, you’re helping build a more secure world,...

item.title: Inside CrowdStrike Automated Leads: A Transformative Approach to Threat Detections
Skoda Data Breach Hits Online Shop Customers
item.description: Using a vulnerability in the portal, hackers accessed names, addresses, email addresses, and phone numbers. The post Skoda Data Breach Hits Online Shop Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Your Purple Team Isn't Purple — It's Just Red and Blue in the Same Room
item.description: Defending a network at 2 am looks a lot like this: an analyst copy-pasting a hash from a PDF into a SIEM query. A red team script is being rewritten by hand so the blue team can use it. A patch waiting on a change-app...

item.title: Cloudflare Lays Off 1,100 Employees in AI-Driven Restructuring
item.description: The company topped revenue and earnings forecasts for the first quarter of 2026, but its shares plunged more than 20%. The post Cloudflare Lays Off 1,100 Employees in AI-Driven Restructuring appeared first on Security...

item.title: US: FCC Relaxes Foreign-Made Router Ban to Allow for Security Updates
item.description: The same extension applies to security updates shipped to US-based users of foreign-made drones

item.title: SailPoint Discloses GitHub Repository Hack
item.description: The incident occurred on April 20 and did not affect customer data in the company’s production and staging environments. The post SailPoint Discloses GitHub Repository Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Instagram messaging encryption removed, and privacy advocates are pushing back
item.description: After introducing optional end-to-end encrypted messaging in 2023, Instagram announced in March 2026 that encryption for direct messages would be discontinued, and the feature was removed on May 8. The change allows I...

item.title: The questionnaire-based TPRM model is broken, and TrustCloud has a fix
item.description: TrustCloud announced a new version of TrustLens, its third party risk management (TPRM) solution. The new TrustLens agentic AI capabilities focus on delivering four requirements every CISO wants in their TPRM program:...

item.title: ShinyHunters Escalates Canvas Extortion with School by School Ransom Campaign
item.description: ShinyHunters has escalated its Canvas extortion campaign, defacing hundreds of school login pages and threatening to leak stolen data unless institutions negotiate

item.title: The scam economy has found its AI upgrade
item.description: Scam attempts continue to reach consumers via email, text messages, social media, online advertising, and phone calls. The volume of exposure has remained stable over the past year, with more than half of consumers en...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in BerriAI LiteLLM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in BerriAI LiteLLM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a...

item.title: TrickMo Android banker adopts TON blockchain for covert comms
item.description: A new variant of the TrickMo Android banking malware, delivered in campaigns targeting users across Europe, introduces new commands and uses The Open Network (TON) for stealthy command-and-control communications. [...]

item.title: Zara Data Breach Impacts Nearly 200,000 Customers
item.description: ShinyHunters gets away with emails and other data on 200,000 Zara customers

item.title: Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools
item.description: Tens of thousands of students studying for final exams around the world have regained access to a key online learning system after a cyberattack had earlier knocked it offline. The post Canvas System Is Online After a...

item.title: Rustinel: Open-source endpoint detection for Windows and Linux
item.description: Open-source endpoint detection has long been split between Windows-focused tools built around Sysmon and Linux tools built around eBPF or auditd. Defenders running mixed environments have had to stitch together separa...

item.title: Review: Foundations of Cybersecurity, 2nd edition
item.description: Jason Andress has refreshed his introductory security text for No Starch Press. He writes in the introduction that the term security now extends past data center servers to cloud resources, mobile devices, the Interne...

item.title: Security teams are turning to AI to survive alert overload
item.description: The World Economic Forum white paper “Empowering Defenders: AI for Cybersecurity” identified AI as the biggest driver of change in cybersecurity for 94% of survey respondents. The paper found that 77% of organizations...

item.title: Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign
item.description: Victims span across the aviation, critical infrastructure, energy, logistics, public administration, and technology sectors. The post Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign appeared first on Securi...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, May 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9926, (Mon, May 11th)
Security Affairs newsletter Round 576 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
item.description: A new round of the weekly Security Affairs newsletter has arrived! Every week, the best security articles from Security Affairs are free in your email box. Enjoy a new round of the weekly SecurityAffairs newsletter, i...

item.title: Hackers abuse Google ads, Claude.ai chats to push Mac malware
item.description: Attackers are abusing Google Ads and legitimate Claude.ai shared chats in an active malvertising campaign. Users searching for "Claude mac download" may come across sponsored search results that list claude.ai as the...

item.title: Official JDownloader site served malware to Windows and Linux users between May 6 and May 7
item.description: JDownloader website was hacked to distribute malicious Windows and Linux installers carrying a Python RAT between May 6–7, 2026. JDownloader official website was compromised in a supply chain attack that replaced legi...

item.title: Week in review: cPanel vulnerability actively exploited, DigiCert breach, LinkedIn job scams
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Your work apps are quietly handing 19 data points to someone Office work in 2026 relies on mobile apps used alongside p...

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service. The list of vulnerabili...

item.title: Fake OpenAI repository on Hugging Face pushes infostealer malware
item.description: A malicious Hugging Face repository that reached the platform's trending list impersonated OpenAI's "Privacy Filter" project to deliver information-stealing malware to Windows users. [...]

item.title: Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX): A Fileless Linux Implant Built for Stealth and Persistence
item.description: Researchers uncovered QLNX, a Linux RAT targeting developers to steal credentials, log keystrokes, monitor systems, and enable remote access. Security researchers discovered a previously undocumented Linux malware cal...

item.title: Braintrust security incident raises concerns over AI supply chain risks
item.description: Braintrust warned customers to rotate API keys after hackers breached an AWS account, exposing secrets tied to cloud-based AI models. AI observability startup Braintrust warned customers to rotate API keys after attac...

item.title: Copy Fail and DirtyFrag: Linux Page Cache Bugs in the Wild
item.description: This research analyzes the Linux kernel privilege escalation vulnerabilities Copy Fail and DirtyFrag, which exploit subtle page cache corruption bugs to create reliable paths to root access. Additionally, Elastic Secu...

item.title: Critical Apache HTTP Server HTTP/2 Vulnerability Could Enable Remote Code Execution
item.description: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-23918, CVSS 8.8) was disclosed affecting Apache HTTP Server, allowing attackers to potentially achieve remote code execution via specially crafted HTTP/2 requests. Due to the po...

item.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux backdoor named PamDOORa that's being advertised on the Rehub Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 by a threat actor called "darkworm." The backdoor is des...

item.title: RansomHouse says it breached Trellix and exposes internal systems
item.description: RansomHouse claimed responsibility for the Trellix breach, adding the security firm to its Tor data leak site and sharing screenshots of internal systems. The RansomHouse ransomware group has claimed responsibility fo...

item.title: Australian Cyber Security Centre Issues Alert Over ClickFix Attacks
item.description: ACSC warns over a campaign targeting organizations which uses ClickFix to deliver Vidar infostealer malware

item.title: Hackers ate my homework: Educational SaaS Canvas down after cyberattack
item.description: ShinyHunters takes the credit and gives developer an F for security

item.title: Ivanti EPMM vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks (CVE-2026-6973)
item.description: Ivanti has released fixes for 5 high-severity vulnerabilities in its Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) solution, one of which (CVE-2026-6973) has being exploited as a zero-day by attackers. “We are aware of a very limite...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Giant Squid Live in the Waters of Western Australia
item.description: Evidence of them has been found by analyzing DNA in the seawater. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: A Framework for AI Threat Readiness
item.description: AI models now find and exploit zero-days autonomously. This 4-pillar framework accelerates patching, analysis, and threat response.

item.title: Metasploit Wrap-Up 05/08/2026
item.description: Spring cleanup This week’s Metasploit updates focused on foundational improvements and expanded target reach. Key enhancements were made to the recently released Copy Fail exploit module, which now benefits from paylo...

item.title: Insider Betting on Polymarket
item.description: Insider trading is rife on Polymarket: Analysis by the Anti-Corruption Data Collective, a non-profit research and advocacy group, found that long-shot bets—­defined as wagers of $2,500 or more at odds of 35 percent or...

item.title: Worm rubs out competitor's malware, then takes control
item.description: All your compromised credentials are belong to us now instead of the other gang

item.title: NVIDIA confirms GeForce NOW data breach affecting Armenian users
item.description: NVIDIA has confirmed in a statement for BleepingComputer that GeForce NOW user information has been exposed in a data breach. [...]

item.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users into joining a s...

item.title: Why More Analysts Won’t Solve Your SOC’s Alert Problem
item.description: Attackers move faster than overwhelmed SOC teams can realistically investigate alerts. Prophet Security breaks down how AI can help analysts investigate alerts faster and focus on real threats. [...]

item.title: GCSI 2026: AI Readiness in a City Built in Layers
item.description: GCSI 2026 showed why cyber readiness depends on visibility into vendors, AI tools, identities, workflows, and hidden business dependencies.

item.title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
item.description: The attack on the Trellix source code repository disclosed last week has been claimed by the RansomHouse threat group, which leaked a small set of images as proof of the intrusion. [...]

item.title: Zero Chaos: Scaling Detection Engineering at the Speed of Software, with Detection As Code
item.description: Every engineering team in your organization ships code through a pipeline. They branch, test, review, and deploy. If something breaks, they roll back. If someone asks "what changed?", the answer is in the commit histo...

item.title: See and Secure Everything at the Edge with Wiz and Akamai
item.description: Akamai edge configurations are now visible on the Wiz Security Graph, giving teams a single understanding of risk from edge to runtime

item.title: Polish Security Agency Reports ICS Breaches at Five Water Treatment Plants
item.description: The hackers gained the ability to modify equipment operational parameters, creating a direct risk to the public water supply. The post Polish Security Agency Reports ICS Breaches at Five Water Treatment Plants appeare...

item.title: AI Firm Braintrust Prompts API Key Rotation After Data Breach
item.description: Hackers accessed one of the company’s AWS accounts and compromised AI provider secrets stored in Braintrust. The post AI Firm Braintrust Prompts API Key Rotation After Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: One Missed Threat Per Week: What 25M Alerts Reveal About Low-Severity Risk
item.description: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. This is not just anecdotal, but rather backed by a recent report investigating more than...

item.title: Google is turning Android Studio into a policy watchdog
item.description: Google has expanded Play Policy Insights in Android Studio to help developers catch policy issues while coding, including warnings for common problems such as missing login credentials. Later this year, developers who...

item.title: Helping North Korean IT remote workers is becoming a fast track to prison
item.description: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison for operating “laptop farms” that helped North Korean IT workers gain employment at nearly 70 American companies, generating more than $1.2 million for Pyongyan...

item.title: Former govt contractor convicted for wiping dozens of federal databases
item.description: A 34-year-old Virginia man was found guilty of conspiring to destroy dozens of government databases after getting fired from his job as a federal contractor. [...]

item.title: ‘PCPJack’ Worm Removes TeamPCP Infections, Steals Credentials
item.description: The malware framework targets web applications and cloud environments, including AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, and more. The post ‘PCPJack’ Worm Removes TeamPCP Infections, Steals Credentials appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Avantra’s new AI can diagnose SAP failures in seconds
item.description: Avantra launched Avantra 26, an advancement in AI-driven operations, strengthening native integration with SAP Cloud ALM, and delivering automated visibility across SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Avantra also...

item.title: Securonix launches AI threat research agent and ThreatWatch validation tool
item.description: Securonix announced the Securonix Threat Research Agent and ThreatWatch for ThreatQ, expanding how security teams research threats, validate exposure, and turn intelligence into documented action. Built on the ThreatQ...

item.title: OpenAI tunes GPT-5.5-Cyber for more permissive security workflows
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out GPT-5.5-Cyber, a variant of its latest AI model, in limited preview for verified cybersecurity professionals and organizations through its Trusted Access for Cyber program. Trusted Access for Cyb...

item.title: AI, Cyberwarfare, and Autonomous Weapons: Inside America’s New Military Strategy
item.description: The Pentagon is integrating AI into military operations, transforming cybersecurity, targeting, and command systems into a unified warfare architecture. May 2026 marks a turning point in the evolution of modern warfar...

item.title: Transilience AI unveils Security Operating System for cloud remediation
item.description: Transilience AI has announced the general availability of its Full Stack Security Operating System for the cloud, platform designed to solve one of enterprise security’s most persistent challenges: bridging the gap be...

item.title: Object First Fleet Manager simplifies distributed backup storage
item.description: Object First released Object First Fleet Manager, a cloud-based service that simplifies the management of distributed Ootbi backup storage deployments for Veeam Software environments. Built for enterprises and service...

item.title: Vulnerability in Claude Extension for Chrome Exposes AI Agent to Takeover
item.description: Lax extension permissions and improper trust implementation allow attackers to inject prompts in the Claude Chrome extension. The post Vulnerability in Claude Extension for Chrome Exposes AI Agent to Takeover appeared...

item.title: New infosec products of the week: May 8, 2026
item.description: Here’s a look at the most interesting products from the past week LastPass, Operant AI, Sysdig, and VIAVI. Operant AI Endpoint Protector secures AI agents and MCP tools Operant AI has launched Operant Endpoint Protect...

item.title: Detecting Web Server Probing & Fuzzing in Traefik with Automated Cloudflare Response
item.description: This article shows how a customized Elastic Security ES|QL detection rule can identify web server probing and fuzzing activity in Traefik logs and automatically block the attacking IP via Cloudflare.

item.title: Kubernetes security fundamentals: Secrets
item.description: A look at how to secure Kubernetes secrets

item.title: Cyber resiliency in practice: Lessons from recent supply chain attacks
item.description: Learn how modern cyber resiliency helps organizations prevent, detect, and recover from supply chain attacks like Trivy, Axios, and LiteLLM.

item.title: PAN-OS RCE Exploit Under Active Use Enabling Root Access and Espionage
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed that threat actors may have attempted to unsuccessfully exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw as early as April 9, 2026. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0300 (C...

item.title: Palo Alto Zero-Day Exploited in Campaign Bearing Hallmarks of Chinese State Hacking
item.description: The cybersecurity firm has not explicitly accused China of being behind the attack, but the evidence suggests it was. The post Palo Alto Zero-Day Exploited in Campaign Bearing Hallmarks of Chinese State Hacking appear...

item.title: New TCLBanker malware self-spreads over WhatsApp and Outlook
item.description: A new trojan named TCLBanker, which targets 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms, uses a trojanized MSI installer for Logitech AI Prompt Builder to infect systems. [...]

item.title: Cisco patches high-severity flaws enabling SSRF, code execution attacks
item.description: Cisco fixed several high‑severity flaws in its enterprise products, including SSRF bugs in Unity Connection that could enable code execution or service disruption. Cisco released patches for multiple high‑severity vul...

item.title: Rapid7 and OpenAI: Helping Defenders Move at Machine Speed
item.description: Wade Woolwine is Senior Director, Product Security at Rapid7. Announcing OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber program CIOs and CISOs are telling us the same thing in different ways: Advances in frontier AI are accelerati...

item.title: Worries About AI’s Risks to Humanity Loom Over the Trial Pitting Musk Against OpenAI’s Leaders
item.description: Musk said that he could have founded OpenAI as a for-profit company, just like the other companies he started or took over. “I deliberately chose this,” he said, “for the public good.” The post Worries About AI’s Risk...

item.title: ICYMI: April 2026 @AWS Security
item.description: Read all about the latest AWS security features, compliance updates, and hands-on resources in our new, monthly digest posts. You’ll find expert blog posts, new service capabilities, code samples, and workshops. AWS S...

item.title: Unplug your way to better code
item.description: Cybersecurity concepts — logs, packets, DNS exfiltration, and more — are usually intangible, and its practitioners are prone to mental fatigue, Amy takes a second to yell at you to go touch grass.

item.title: What Mozilla learned running an AI security bug hunting pipeline on Firefox
item.description: Over the past several months, Mozilla ran an agentic harness powered by Claude Mythos Preview across Firefox’s source code, identifying 271 security bugs that were fixed in Firefox 150, with additional fixes shipped i...

item.title: $250 million cryptocurrency heist funded luxury fashion, nightclub parties, and private jets
item.description: 20-year-old California resident Marlon Ferro, known online as “GothFerrari,” was sentenced to 78 months in prison for his role in a cryptocurrency theft operation tied to more than $250 million in stolen digital asset...

item.title: One keypress is all it takes to compromise four AI coding tools
item.description: Developers clone unfamiliar repositories all the time. Open-source projects, work from teammates, sample code from a tutorial, a library someone recommended on a forum. The convention is old and reasonable: you look a...

item.title: Node.js 26 ships with Temporal API enabled by default
item.description: Developers managing JavaScript runtimes have a new major version to evaluate. Node.js 26.0.0 brings the long-awaited Temporal API to the platform alongside an updated V8 engine, a refreshed HTTP client, and several lo...

item.title: Facial recognition arrives at the gates of Disney’s magic kingdom
item.description: Disney has equipped select entrance lanes at Disneyland Park and Disney California Adventure Park with facial recognition technology, saying the system is intended to streamline re-entry procedures and help prevent fr...

item.description: Kloudfuse has announced the general availability of Kloudfuse 4.0. The release helps enterprises meet rising compliance requirements, adopt AI-driven observability with production-grade governance, and scale their obs...

item.description: Red Hat has announced the upcoming general availability of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.2 and 9.8. Building on the innovation of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10, the latest versions help address security threats, speed AI...

item.title: AWS achieves SNI 27017, SNI 27018, and SNI 9001 certifications for the AWS Asia Pacific (Jakarta) Region
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) achieved three Standar Nasional Indonesia (SNI) certifications for the AWS Asia Pacific (Jakarta) Region: SNI ISO/IEC 27017:2015, SNI ISO/IEC 27018:2019, and SNI ISO 9001:2015. SNI represents...

item.title: Boost Security Raises $4 Million for SDLC Defense Platform
item.description: The company is expanding its platform’s capabilities with the acquisition of SecureIQx and Korbit.ai. The post Boost Security Raises $4 Million for SDLC Defense Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Legacy Security Tools Are Failing Data Protection, Capital One Software Report Finds
item.description: Traditional network security tools are undermining data protection, with Forrester and Capital One Software research warning AI adoption is impossible without rethinking data security

item.title: Claude Code OAuth Tokens Can Be Stolen Through Stealthy MCP Hijacking
item.description: Mitiga researchers say attackers can silently redirect Claude Code MCP traffic, intercept OAuth tokens, and maintain persistent access to connected SaaS platforms. The post Claude Code OAuth Tokens Can Be Stolen Throu...

item.title: Cline Kanban Flaw Lets Websites Hijack AI Coding Agents
item.description: Oasis Security finds critical Cline kanban WebSocket flaw exposing AI coding agents to hijack

item.title: Chrome 148 Rolls Out With 127 Security Fixes
item.description: The fresh browser update resolves critical-severity integer overflow and use-after-free vulnerabilities. The post Chrome 148 Rolls Out With 127 Security Fixes appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: The Browser Is Breaking Your DLP: How Data Slips Past Modern Controls
item.description: Your security controls aren't failing, they're missing where most of today's work actually happens. Keep Aware shows how browser activity like copy/paste and AI prompts bypass traditional protections. [...]

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. In 20...

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic LLMs Used in Critical Infrastructure Cyber-Attack, Warns Dragos
item.description: Commercial AI models were used to help plan and conduct cyber-attack against operational technology of a water and drainage facility, say researchers

item.title: Attackers Could Exploit AI Vision Models Using Imperceptible Image Changes
item.description: Cisco’s AI security researchers have analyzed ways to target vision-language models (VLMs) using pixel-level perturbation. The post Attackers Could Exploit AI Vision Models Using Imperceptible Image Changes appeared f...

item.title: Why Security in 2026 Requires Continuous Threat and Exposure Management (CTEM) at Scale
item.description: Let's be honest, the patching window just shrank to something no practitioner or organization can keep up with. Organizations now need to operate in an environment that must assume breach, which means fundamentals lik...

item.title: AI Coding Agents Could Fuel Next Supply Chain Crisis
item.description: “TrustFall” attack shows how AI coding agents can be manipulated into launching stealthy supply chain compromises. The post AI Coding Agents Could Fuel Next Supply Chain Crisis appeared first on SecurityWeek .
